findings.title: Critical Need for Kafka Migration
findings.description: The current use of Apache Kafka 2.8 poses significant risks due to its end-of-life status and known vulnerabilities. Customers are experiencing increased instability, which is impacting their operations. Migration to Kafka 3.7+ is essential to ensure platform reliability and security.
assumptions: Customers rely heavily on Kafka for message brokering.
Instability in Kafka will lead to operational issues.

evidence.excerpt: Core message broker runs Kafka 2.8 which reaches end-of-life in Q1 2026.
We've noticed increasing instability in the Kafka layer — consumer rebalances are taking 10x longer than a year ago.
Risk Level: Critical; Impact: Core message broker for entire platform.

recommendations: Prioritize migration planning to Kafka 3.7+ with KRaft mode.

findings.title: Vendor Lock-in Risk with ETL SDK
findings.description: The reliance on VendorX's proprietary ETL SDK creates a financial risk due to an impending price increase. Customers are concerned about the sustainability of their current solution and are seeking alternatives to avoid vendor lock-in.
assumptions: Customers are sensitive to pricing changes.

evidence.excerpt: VendorX was acquired last quarter and the new parent company announced a 3x price increase effective January 2026.
If the price triples, we'll need to migrate regardless.
Risk Level: Critical (financial); Impact: Transformation layer for all data pipelines.

recommendations: Evaluate open-source alternatives like Apache Beam and plan migration.

findings.title: Python 3.9 EOL Migration Requirement
findings.description: The impending end-of-life for Python 3.9 necessitates an upgrade to Python 3.12 across multiple services. This presents technical challenges due to dependency incompatibilities that need to be addressed to maintain security and functionality.
assumptions: Customers require ongoing support and security updates for their technology stack.

evidence.excerpt: Pipeline worker services run Python 3.9 which reaches end-of-life October 2025.
Python 3.9 EOL means no more security patches, which violates their vendor security policy.
Risk Level: High; Impact: 8 pipeline worker services, internal ML library.

recommendations: Plan and execute the migration to Python 3.12, addressing dependency blockers.

findings.title: Need for Dependency Health Dashboard
findings.description: There is a lack of visibility into the health of dependencies across the platform, leading to reactive management of vulnerabilities. A centralized dashboard would enable proactive monitoring and timely remediation of issues.
assumptions: Customers prefer proactive over reactive management of dependencies.

evidence.excerpt: There is no centralized view of dependency health across the platform.

recommendations: Develop a dependency health dashboard with automated alerting.

summary: The analysis reveals critical risks associated with deprecated technologies and vendor dependencies in the DataPipe platform. Key insights highlight the urgent need for migration strategies for Kafka and Python, as well as addressing vendor lock-in with the ETL SDK. Additionally, a proactive approach to dependency management through a health dashboard is recommended to mitigate future risks.
